chunk:
### Taping Procedure: Full Knee Taping

| Step | Application Path | Position | Stretch % |
|------|------------------|----------|-----------|
| 1 | Apply a small “I” strip across the patellar tendon | Long sitting, knee at 90° flexion | 50–75% |
| 2 | Apply an “I” strip from the lateral aspect of the thigh toward the patella, then 50% stretch from lateral side of patella to anterior knee, finish by crossing tibial tuberosity with no stretch at the end | Long sitting, knee at 90° flexion | 25% (thigh to patella), 50% (lateral patella to anterior knee), 0% (end) |
| 3 | Apply an “I” strip from the medial aspect of the thigh toward the patella, then 50% stretch from medial side of patella to front of knee, crossing tibial tuberosity with no stretch at the end | Long sitting, knee at 90° flexion | 25% (thigh to patella), 50% (medial patella to front of knee), 0% (end) |
| 4 | Heat activate the glue | — | — |

> **Figure 3.5**: Small “I” strip applied across the patellar tendon.

> **Figure 3.6**: An “I” strip is applied from the lateral aspect of the thigh to the tibial tuberosity.

> **Figure 3.7**: An “I” strip is applied from the medial aspect of the thigh to the tibial tuberosity.

Knee pain (patellofemoral syndrome/Osgood-Schlatter’s disease)
